food_name: Black Pepper Beef Steak (Western Hawker, Sizzling Plate)

Hēi Jiāo Niú Pá Pèi Shǔ Tiáo

english: Black Pepper Beef Steak (Western Hawker, Sizzling Plate)
chinese: Hēi Jiāo Niú Pá Pèi Shǔ Tiáo
romanized: Hēi Jiāo Niú Pá Pèi Shǔ Tiáo

food_subgroup: Beef steak (rib-eye, sirloin, or rump) grilled on cast-iron hot plate, served sizzling with black pepper sauce, thick-cut fries, and salad

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has no concept for Singapore hawker black pepper beef steak. Closest parent is a beef steak with sauce preparation (FOODON:03315595 — beef steak food product). No exact URI assigned to avoid fabrication.
ontology.foodex2_description: Beef meat, cooked — used as closest parent for Singapore Western hawker black pepper beef steak with fries and salad on sizzling plate
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No FoodEx2 code exists for the composite Singapore hawker black pepper beef steak plate. A0BDF (cooked beef meat) is the closest defensible parent anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept maps to Singapore hawker black pepper beef steak as a composite dish.

preparation_method: served on preheated cast-iron sizzling plate
black pepper sauce poured over at service

serving.natural_language_portion: One full hawker sizzling plate comprising approximately 180g beef steak, 200g thick-cut fries, 100g black pepper sauce, and 100g salad or vegetables, totalling 580g.

nutrient_source.title: Black Pepper Beef Steak (Western Hawker) — HPB Singapore Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Black Pepper Beef Steak (Western Hawker) used as primary source. Values represent the composite dish including beef, black pepper sauce, fries, and salad per 100g edible portion.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of French Fries and Mixed Beef Plate — University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry exists for Singapore hawker black pepper beef steak. GI of 68 (medium) is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database for thick-cut fried potato (GI ~65–72), which is the dominant carbohydrate contributor in this composite dish (200g fries out of 580g total plate). The beef steak component has negligible GI contribution. Glycemic load per 580g serving calculated as: (68 × 84.1g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 57.2, rounded to 57.3. Confidence 0.9.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content — 428mg per 100g; a full 580g serving delivers approximately 2,482mg sodium, approaching or exceeding the WHO daily limit of 2,000mg in a single meal
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should request 'less sauce' when ordering black pepper beef steak, as soy sauce in the marinade and black pepper sauce is the primary sodium driver; requesting unsalted fries and additional salad without dressing can reduce sodium intake by an estimated 30–40%.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load per serving (GL 57.3) driven by 200g thick-cut fries contributing approximately 84g available carbohydrate; the beef steak component is glycaemically neutral and protein-rich
type2_diabetes.guidance: Patients with Type 2 Diabetes should substitute fries with additional salad or steamed vegetables where available, eat the beef steak first to leverage protein-mediated glucose attenuation, and limit fry consumption to no more than 50–80g per sitting to keep the glycemic load below 20.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate saturated fat (3.5g/100g; ~20.3g per full serving) from beef and butter in sauce, combined with high sodium (2,482mg per serving); trans fat is low at 0.1g/100g
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should limit portion size to half a plate (approximately 290g), request the sauce on the side to reduce saturated fat and sodium intake, and choose leaner cuts such as sirloin or rump over rib-eye when available.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Beneficial nutrients: high zinc (3.2mg/100g), vitamin B12 (1.8mcg/100g), selenium (18mcg/100g) from beef; piperine from black pepper enhances bioavailability of co-consumed anti-inflammatory polyphenols
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: This dish is conditionally suitable for patients with rheumatoid arthritis due to its high zinc and selenium content from beef and the bioavailability-enhancing effect of piperine; the primary inflammatory concern is the high glycemic load from fries, which should be minimised.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Black Pepper Beef Steak at a Singapore hawker Western stall is one of the most recognisable dishes in the kopitiam repertoire. The dish arrives at the table on a preheated cast-iron sizzling plate, steaming and crackling dramatically as the black pepper sauce makes contact with the hot iron surface. The steak — typically rib-eye, sirloin, or rump, weighing 150–180g — is marinated in soy sauce and garlic, then grilled on a flat griddle before being transferred to the sizzling plate. Black pepper sauce is made from cracked whole peppercorns, butter, a splash of cream or cornstarch-thickened stock, soy sauce, garlic, and sliced onion, and is poured over the steak at the table. Thick-cut fries and a simple green salad complete the plate. The composite dish (580g full plate) delivers 195 kcal per 100g, with 13.5g protein, 10.5g fat, and 14.5g carbohydrate per 100g — the carbohydrate load is dominated by the fries. Sodium is high at 428mg/100g (approximately 2,482mg per full serving), making this a high-sodium meal. The glycemic index is approximately 68 (medium), driven by the thick-cut fries, with a glycemic load of 57.3 per full serving — clinically significant for patients with Type 2 Diabetes. The beef component contributes excellent zinc (3.2mg/100g), vitamin B12 (1.8mcg/100g), and selenium (18mcg/100g). Piperine from black pepper at the concentrations used in hawker black pepper sauce is documented to enhance the bioavailability of co-consumed polyphenols, making this an unexpectedly anti-inflammatory dish for its genre. This dish is a Singapore Western hawker adaptation of European steak traditions, and the sizzling cast-iron hot plate presentation is a quintessential signature of Singapore Western stalls found in kopitiams and hawker centres across the island.

confidence_reason: Primary nutrient data sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database, which is the approved primary source for Singapore dishes. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database using thick-cut fried potato as the closest analogous food, which is the dominant carbohydrate contributor. All mandatory multilingual names present. Ontology anchored to FoodEx2 A0BDF as closest parent with documented gap note.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractionation data for this composite Singapore hawker dish (beef steak + black pepper sauce + fries + salad) is not separately reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of April 2026. water_g set to -1: moisture content for this composite dish is not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database. trans_fat_g is 0.1g/100g as reported by HPB Singapore Foodo — this is a low but non-zero value consistent with trace trans fats from beef (naturally occurring conjugated linoleic acid and vaccenic acid) and any partially hydrogenated frying oil used for fries. GI of 68 is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database data for thick-cut fried potato (GI 65–72); no direct GI measurement exists for this composite dish. Glycemic load of 57.3 per 580g serving is clinically high and the primary dietary concern for patients with Type 2 Diabetes. Sodium of 2,482mg per full serving approaches or exceeds the WHO recommended daily limit of 2,000mg in a single meal.
